text:
Department of Neurology

Clinic Date: 19 November 2015

Dr Patel
City General Hospital
Department of Neurology, 12 Bridge Street, Manchester M1 4AB

Dear Dr Patel,

Aleksandar Petrov, DOB: 17-03-1991, Hospital No: M938472 NHS No. 9045612378
Flat 3B, 22 Brookside Court, Manchester, M14 6QP

Diagnoses:

Recurrent seizures of uncertain aetiology under investigation.

Current Medication:

Levetiracetam 1,000 mg twice daily
Vitamin D 1,000 IU daily

Plan:

- Continue current dose of levetiracetam
- Safety counselling reinforced; provide written seizure first aid materials in preferred language
- Arrange routine bloods (U&E, FBC, LFTs) and levetiracetam level if clinically indicated
- Community epilepsy nurse to support with education and NHS service navigation
- Interpreter to be booked for future appointments as needed
- Follow up in 6 months, sooner if any events recur

I reviewed Aleksandar Petrov in clinic today with interpreter support. They report no further episodes since the medication dose was adjusted several months ago, and there have been no blackouts, injuries, or witnessed events during this period. Sleep has been more regular and they have reduced alcohol intake. They are settling into local services and learning how to access NHS urgent and routine care; they find written information helpful when provided in their preferred language.

Neurological examination today was unremarkable. Previous basic bloods were within reference limits; further investigations will be guided by clinical course. We discussed SUDEP awareness, driving regulations, and lifestyle triggers. There are no current red flags. Given stable control and good adherence, I recommend continuing levetiracetam at the present dose. If they remain well, we will review again in six months. They were advised to contact us via the epilepsy nurse line if any events recur or for medication concerns.

With best wishes,

Yours sincerely,

entities.text: no further episodes since the medication dose was adjusted several months ago

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states the patient has had no further episodes since the medication dose was adjusted several months ago. There is no precise start date for seizure freedom and no confirmation that it has been at least 6 months. Therefore, per the rules, when seizure-free duration is present but the 6-month criterion cannot be confirmed, label as 'seizure free for multiple months.' No other seizure frequencies are provided.
seizure_frequency_number: no further episodes since the medication dose was adjusted several months ago
reference: Seizure-free after dose escalation of ASM
